behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive error messages
behaviors.justification: Pure function: takes a cron string, returns valid schedule object or throws error. No I/O, no external dependencies. All bugs (invalid month, day, malformed syntax) are caught by providing invalid inputs and verifying error messages. A mock cannot miss this — the failure is in parsing logic, not runtime behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterized unit tests: provide invalid expressions (month=13, day=32, incomplete fields, garbage syntax) and verify descriptive errors are raised with clear indication of what's wrong. Also test valid expressions pass.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for cron expressions including edge cases like 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday'
behaviors.justification: Deterministic calculation given known inputs (cron expression + start time). No I/O, no system calls, no external state. Edge cases (Feb 31, leap-year boundaries, month-end logic, weekday arithmetic) are tested by providing specific inputs and verifying output. A mock would not miss failures in boundary logic or leap-year handling — these are pure algorithmic bugs caught by Unit tests.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests with parametrized inputs: schedule for 'last day of month' and verify next run crosses month boundary correctly; test 'nearest weekday' on dates near weekends; verify leap-year transitions (Feb 28/29), month-end boundaries (31st when next month has 30 days). Assert exact timestamp correctness.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector identifies when two schedules would fire within the configured minimum gap and reports conflicting time windows
behaviors.justification: Pure algorithmic comparison: takes two schedule objects and a gap threshold (e.g., 5 minutes), returns boolean + conflict windows. No I/O, no scheduling infrastructure, no external calls. The failure boundary is in comparison logic (off-by-one, wrong operator, missed edge case). A mock that provides two schedules is fully realistic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests: create pairs of schedules with known next-run times; test cases where schedules fire 3 minutes apart (should conflict with 5-min gap), 5 minutes apart (boundary), 6 minutes apart (should not conflict). Verify reported conflict windows are accurate. Test edge case where both fire at exact same time.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling produces correct run times across DST transitions (spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity)
behaviors.justification: Deterministic calculation using real timezone library (IANA database). Spring-forward (2:30 AM doesn't exist) and fall-back (1:30 AM occurs twice) are handled through standard library objects (ZoneInfo, pytz). Unit test using real timezone objects captures the failure: if code doesn't correctly adjust for non-existent times or disambiguate ambiguous times, it will produce wrong output. A mock that just says 'DST=true' would miss subtle bugs, but using real timezone library at Unit level does not.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests with real timezone library: (1) Schedule a job for 2:30 AM US/Eastern on 2024-03-10 (spring forward), verify next-run adjusts to 3:30 AM. (2) Schedule for 1:30 AM US/Eastern on 2024-11-03 (fall back), verify the algorithm disambiguates correctly (e.g., uses 'fold' parameter in zoneinfo). Test in multiple timezones with different DST rules. Verify calculations are consistent across timezone changes.

behaviors.description: When next run time has already passed, engine correctly applies the missed-run policy (skip or backfill) based on job configuration
behaviors.justification: Policy logic is deterministic time comparison. The failure boundary is in the decision logic (should this old run be skipped or backfilled?). Unit test mocks 'current time' to simulate system downtime — if scheduled time is 2025-01-01 10:00 AM and current time is mocked as 2025-01-02, the logic must correctly apply skip vs. backfill policy. A mock of 'now' is fully realistic and sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests: mock current time to be past the calculated next-run-time; verify that skip policy causes job to be marked skipped (next run computed afresh), and backfill policy enqueues missed executions. Test with multiple missed intervals to verify backfill count is correct. Test boundary case where next-run-time is exactly 'now'.
